speaker: Narrator
text: Every world keeps its secrets. Aetheria keeps more than most.

speaker: Narrator
text: Fourteen years ago, a boy died in the rain of another world.

speaker: Narrator
text: He was born again here, to a farmer and his wife, in a village called Briar Glen.

speaker: Narrator
text: He remembers none of it. Only the dream. Rain. Lights. A voice.

speaker: Narrator
text: Today the village celebrates the First Harvest.

speaker: Narrator
text: And by nightfall, everything he loves will be taken from him.

speaker: Narrator
text: Wake up, lad. Your story starts now.

speaker: Mira
text: There you are. I was beginning to think you planned to sleep through the festival.

speaker: Kael
text: I had the dream again.

speaker: Mira
text: The city of glass?

speaker: Kael
text: And the rain.

speaker: Daren
text: Dreams feel important until breakfast.

speaker: Mira
text: Your father believes food is the cure for everything.

speaker: Daren
text: It usually is.

speaker: Mira
text: Kael, the festival won't wait for daydreams. I need supplies before noon.

speaker: Mira
text: Gather five sunroot from the garden, three eggs from the pen, and take the basket to the baker.

speaker: Mira
text: Sunroot from the garden, eggs from the pen, and the basket goes to the baker.

speaker: Mira
text: And mind General Cluck. That bird has opinions.

speaker: Kael
text: Father, what's that bracer you keep repairing?

speaker: Daren
text: Old militia equipment. Nothing more.

speaker: Baker Hobb
text: Ah, the Rowan boy! Is that Mira's festival basket?

speaker: Baker Hobb
text: Wonderful. The harvest loaves will be ready before the music starts.

speaker: Baker Hobb
text: Here, take a fresh loaf for your trouble. Enjoy the festival!

speaker: Baker Hobb
text: Morning, lad! Busy day ahead. Come back if you've got something for me.
